background: Given $n=100$ with 25 successes, and 75 failures, an $80 \%$ two-sided confidence limits on $p$ can be obtained by solving the following equations simultaneously.

task.query: Solve the equation $\sum_{i=25}^{100}\binom{100}{i} p_L^i\left(1-p_L\right)^{100-i}=0.10$ to find the lower confidence limit $p_L$.
Solve the equation $\sum_{i=0}^{24}\binom{100}{i} p_U^i\left(1-p_U\right)^{100-i}=0.10$ to find the upper confidence limit $p_U$.

task.answer: The confidence interval $[0.194, 0.313]$ corresponds to an $80\%$ confidence level.
